title: Bomb the base

content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, there are NNN houses numbered from 111 to NNN, ithi^{th}ith house has a defence system having strength AiA_iAi​.

Chef suspects a bomb drop on **one** of the houses very soon. A bomb with attack strength XXX can destroy the ithi^{th}ith house, if the defence system of the ithi^{th}ith house AiA_iAi​, is **strictly less** than XXX.

Also, when the ithi^{th}ith house is destroyed due to the bomb, all houses with indices jjj such that 1≤j<i1 \leq j < i1≤j<i get destroyed as well irrespective of their defence system.

Given **one** bomb with attack strength XXX, find the **maximum** number of houses that can get destroyed.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains 222 integers N,XN , XN,X.
Second line of test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the maximum number of houses that can get destroyed if the bomb can hit any house.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X — the number of houses and the bomb's attack strength. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, where Ai is the defence strength of the i‑th house.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer — the maximum possible number of houses that can be destroyed when the bomb can be dropped on any one house.

solution.approach: The bomb destroys house i only if Ai < X. When it destroys house i, all houses with indices smaller than i are also destroyed, so the total destroyed equals i. Therefore we only need the largest index i such that Ai < X. Scanning the array from left to right and remembering the last position where Ai < X gives the answer in O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
